data.title: Barramundi Group Ltd: Commencement of the Subscription Period for the Subsequent Offering
data.description:
NOT FOR RELEASE, PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTION, IN WHOLE OR IN PART, DIRECTLY ORINDIRECTLY, IN OR INTO AUSTRALIA, CANADA, HONG KONG, JAPAN OR THE UNITEDSTATESOR ANY OTHER JURISDICTION IN WHICH THE RELEASE, PUBLICATION OR DISTRIBUTIONWOULD BE UNLAWFUL. THIS ANNOUNCEMENT DOES NOT CONSTITUTE AN OFFER OF ANY OFTHESECURITIES DESCRIBED HEREIN.

a) Singapore, 5 September 2025, Barramundi Group Ltd. (the "Company")(EURONEXT:BARRA). Reference is made to the stock exchange notices dated 2 September 2025with minutes of the extraordinary general meeting held on 1 September 2025(the"EGM"), pursuant to which it was resolved, as part of the implementation ofthescheme of arrangement sanctioned by the General Division of the High Court ofthe Republic of Singapore on 14 July 2025, to complete a private placement andasubsequent offering of ordinary shares in the capital of the Company (the"Subsequent Offering").

b) The Subsequent Offering consists of an offering of up to 45,476,503 newordinary shares in the Company (the "Offer Shares"), directed towards theEligible Remaining Shareholders (as defined below) as of end of trading on 1September 2025 (and as registered with the VPS on 3 September 2025) (the"RecordDate").

c) Eligible shareholders will be shareholders of the Company as at theRecordDate who are (i) not subscribers in the private placement referred in the EGMminutes and in the notice to the EGM (as announced on 15 August 2025) and (ii)not resident in a jurisdiction where such offering would be unlawful or wouldrequire any filing, registration or similar action of a registration documentorprospectus ("Eligible Remaining Shareholders").

d) The subscription period for the Subsequent Offering commences today, 5September 2025 at 09:00 hours (CEST) and will end on 12 September 2025 at16:30hours (CEST) (the "Subscription Period"). e) The subscription price per Offer Share is SGD 0.0289 (equivalent toapproximately NOK 0.231) (the "Subscription Price"), which is equal to thesubscription price per ordinary share to be subscribed for by the subscribersinthe private placement referred in the EGM minutes and in the notice to the EGM(as announced on 15 August 2025).

f) Under the Subsequent Offering, each Eligible Remaining Shareholderwill, foreach ordinary share held by it on the Record Date, have the right to subscribefor 1.77566 Offer Shares, rounded down to the nearest whole share. Such rightwill be non-transferable.

g) Eligible Remaining Shareholders are to note that the intention of theSubsequent Offering is to give Eligible Remaining Shareholders the opportunityto subscribe for new shares at the same price as the investors in the privateplacement and also maintain their pro rata ownership in the Company. h) Subscription without allocation rights will not be permitted.

i) Correctly completed share subscription forms (the "Subscription Forms")inthe form attached together with this announcement must be received by theCompany at the following email address: investors@barramundi.com, no laterthan16:30 hours (CEST) on 12 September 2025. Any incomplete or improperlycompletedSubscription Form may be rejected by the Company.

j) An Eligible Remaining Shareholder shall be entitled to direct bywrittennotice to the Company that the Offer Shares subscribed by it pursuant to theSubsequent Offering shall be issued and registered in the name of any nomineeorcustodian holding ordinary shares in the capital of the Company on its behalfasbare nominee (each, a "Nominee"), provided always that such Nominee shall be aperson who as at the Record Date be registered in the register of members oftheCompany as the holder of ordinary shares in the capital of the Company onbehalfof such Eligible Remaining Shareholder. An Eligible Remaining Shareholder maygive such written notice of appointment of a Nominee by completing therelevantportion of the Eligible Remaining Shareholder's Subscription Form anddeliveringthe same to the Company in accordance with subparagraph i) above. Anyincompleteor improperly completed notice of appointment of a Nominee will be rejected bythe Company.

k) The subscription amount shall be paid in cash and in full. Paymentshall bemade by way of telegraphic transfer in immediately available funds to theCompany's bank account, details of which are set out below, and subscribersshall be liable for all bank charges:

Account Name: BARRAMUNDI GROUP LTD Account Number: 033-902216-0 Bank Name: DBS BANK LTD Bank Address: 12 Marina Boulevard, MBFC, Tower 3, Singapore 018982 International Clearing Code / Swift Code: DBSSSGSG

l) Notifications of allocated Offer Shares and the correspondingsubscriptionamount to be paid by each subscribing Eligible Remaining Shareholder areexpected to be distributed on or about 16 September 2025. Payment for theallocated Offer Shares falls due on 18 September 2025 in accordance with thepayment procedures described in subparagraph k) above.

m) The Offer Shares will entitle the holder thereof to all dividends,rights,allotment and other distributions on and from the date of the allotment andissuance of the Offer Shares in accordance with Singapore laws. The allotmentand issuance of the Offer Shares is expected to take place on or around 23September 2025, and such newly issued Offer Shares are expected to be admittedto trading on Euronext on or around 29 September 2025.

Advisors Allen & Gledhill LLP is acting as Singapore legal counsel to the Company.Wikborg Rein Advokatfirma AS is acting as Norwegian legal counsel to theCompany.

Disclosure regulationThis information is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuant to section5-12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.

For further inquiries, please contact:

James KwanChief Executive Officer

Vanessa TanChief Financial Officer

Email: investors@barramundi.com

https://newsweb.oslobors.no/message/654759

data.title: Indian IT stocks slide as speculative tariff buzz triggers sharp reversal
data.description:
** India's information technology index reverses course on Friday, falling 1.5% after gaining 0.5% earlier in the session, dragging benchmarks lower

** The drop begins around 10:07 a.m. IST, shortly after unverified social media posts suggest the U.S. may explore tariffs on Indian IT

** "The decline is not driven by fundamentals but by a speculative report," says Kranthi Bathini, director of equity strategy at Wealthmills Securities

** IT index is down 1.4% this week, underperforming the benchmark Nifty 50's 1.1% rise, exchange data shows

data.title: UK's Berkeley reaffirms profit forecast for fiscal 2026-2027
data.description:
British homebuilder Berkeley reaffirmed its pre-tax profit forecast for fiscal years 2026 and 2027 on Friday and reported stable trading over the first four months of fiscal 2026.

The firm expects its pre-tax profits to be broadly evenly split between the first and second half of the year ending April 30, 2026.

data.title: Norway Manufacturing Output Stalls in July
data.description:
Norway’s manufacturing production stalled month-on-month in July 2025, following a 0.1% decline in the previous month.

Output rose at a faster pace in several sectors, including food, beverages and tobacco (2.0% vs 0.3% in June), machinery and equipment (0.6% vs 0.5%), and ships, boats and oil platforms (3.3% vs 0.8%).

At the same time, the contraction in the output of refined petroleum, chemicals and pharmaceuticals eased (-2.6% vs -3.3%).

Meanwhile, output tumbled for basic metals (-2.9% vs 2.8%).

On a yearly basis, manufacturing output increased by 1.1% in July 2025.

data.title: German industrial orders unexpectedly drop in July
data.description:
German industrial orders unexpectedly fell in July due to a drop in large-scale orders, data showed on Friday.

German industrial orders declined by 2.9% on the previous month on a seasonally and calendar-adjusted basis, the federal statistics office said on Friday.

A Reuters poll of analysts had pointed to a rise of 0.5%.

When large-scale orders are excluded, new orders were 0.7% higher than in the previous month.

The less volatile three-month on three-month comparison showed that new orders in the period from May to July were 0.2% higher than in the previous three months.

After revision of the provisional data, new orders in June decreased by 0.2% on the month, instead of falling by 1.0%. The revision is due to corrected data reported by a large enterprise in the automotive industry.

data.title: Asian FX, stocks rally as dollar retreats on Fed rate cut bets
data.description:
Key points:
  • Asian FX rally on Fed rate cut bets, dollar weak
  • Taiwan dollar, Korean won gain 0.4% each Friday
  • Regional stocks climb led by Manila, Taipei - up 1%

Several Asian currencies gained ground on Friday as the dollar retreated on mounting expectations the Federal Reserve will cut rates this month, while equities climbed after Wall Street's overnight surge to fresh record highs.

South Korea's won and Taiwan's dollar spearheaded the regional advance, each climbing 0.4% against the greenback. The Taiwan dollar rose for a third day after suffering seven consecutive sessions of declines.

In Thailand, parliament was set to chose a new prime minister after a court's dismissal of Paetongtarn Shinawatra as prime minister. The Thai baht strengthened 0.2% and Bangkok equities added 1%.

Overall, however, trade was thin with markets in Malaysia and Indonesia closed for public holidays.

Markets have all but priced in a quarter-point Fed rate cut at next week's policy meeting, with traders expecting 60 basis points of reductions by year-end.

The U.S. dollar index fell 0.2% to 98.11 as traders awaited jobs data later in the day expected to firm up the case for a cut. The Fed is due to meet on September 16 and 17.

"We anticipate the Federal Reserve will ease policy over the coming months, providing room for Asian central banks to cut rates to support growth amid trade headwinds," MUFG forex strategist Lloyd Chan said. "Against a backdrop of a weaker US dollar, we favour the yen, rupiah, and ringgit."

Expectations of an easier monetary environment have supported global equities, and the S&P 500 rose 0.8% on Thursday to finish at a record high.

That helped regional equities post broad gains, led by Manila shares , which rose 1% in their strongest performance in more than a week. Taipei stocks TWSE:TAIEX also climbed 1%, notching their third consecutive session of advances.

Taiwan's benchmark has gained 0.6% for the week, while Manila's index has eked out a 0.1% weekly advance after three straight weeks of declines.

The MSCI gauge of emerging market currencies (.MIEM00000CUS) was little changed this week, while a gauge of emerging Asian equities (.MIMS00000PUS) advanced over 1%, on track for its best week since August 11.

Though Indonesian markets had some respite this week, they could face headwinds again as deadly protests over lawmakers' bonuses escalated after police killed a motorcycle taxi driver during demonstrations.

Rights groups report 10 deaths and over 1,000 injuries in clashes with security forces.

The rupiah gained 0.4% over four sessions on broad dollar weakness after losing more than 1% due to the protests last week. Jakarta stocks rose 0.5% this week, after losing 0.4% last week as investors focused on expected Fed rate cuts.

HIGHLIGHTS:

** Indonesia to discuss fuel supply issue with private distributors

** China's central bank to inject 1 trillion yuan
